🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a pre-revenue European regulated-intermediary SaaS startup building DORA or IDD compliance tooling for insurance distribution at pre-seed stage.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European fintech startup integrating with Open Banking or PSD2 APIs to automate payment reconciliation or AML screening for regulated payment providers.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (14-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 14 total):
ai_workflow_automation weight=3 — 7 entries
in 2d — Wed May 6“Here is a European seed-stage AI workflow automation startup replacing manual document approval or data-entry loops for operations and finance teams in SMEs.”

in 6d — Sun May 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup building AI agents on top of n8n, Make, or Zapier APIs to automate multi-step enterprise workflows.”
in 7d — Mon May 11“Here is a European AI startup adjacent to robotic process automation, applying LLMs to invoice matching, purchase order approval, or expense categorization.”
in 8d — Tue May 12“Here is a European seed-stage AI-native workflow automation startup similar to Dust or Bardeen, targeting knowledge worker productivity in B2B context.”

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURL
Complissimo20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (regulated_intermediary_fintech) fails because while Complissimo offers DORA compliance software for financial institutions, it targets the European market and its headquarters are in Belgium, not France. The thesis specifically requires 'French' regulated intermediaries or 'French SMEs or mid-market companies'.https://complissimo.com
Kalipso90MATCHThe company's core business is an early-stage B2B AI-powered regulatory compliance platform (SaaS) sold to enterprise compliance teams, fulfilling STEP A. It aligns with the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle (STEP B) by providing B2B SaaS for compliance officers automating regulatory reporting and workflows under frameworks like DORA and MiFID II, which includes French regulated intermediaries, though the geographical focus on France is not explicitly stated. The 'AI workflow automation' angle is also met. The company's description and business model confirm a clear B2B SaaS product without being a consulting firm or consumer product.https://kalipso.ai
Regsearch AI30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed for 'ai_workflow_automation' because the company provides expert-led training and consulting services for AI governance and regulatory compliance, not an AI-native workflow automation product. It also fails for 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' because it offers services/training rather than B2B SaaS for French regulated intermediaries.https://regsearch.ai
Dylogy30NO MATCHDylogy offers an AI platform for document analysis in the insurance industry, which aligns with AI workflow automation. However, there is no evidence the company targets French SMEs or mid-market companies, or that it is headquartered in Europe, per the REJECT CRITERIA for [ai_workflow_automation] and the specific angle's requirements for French focus. The description also mentions 'Financial / Insurance Sector' implying large enterprises, not SMEs.https://dylogy.com
Dorhyan.eu20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because the company offers tech-enabled consultancy and services for EU fund passporting and regulatory hosting, which is not a pure B2B software product.https://dorhyan.eu
Finchecker30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (regulated_intermediary_fintech) fails because the company does not specifically target France. The description states global coverage (90+ countries) for its AML/KYC solutions. The thesis requires a focus on French regulated intermediaries.https://finchecker.eu
StarLiX30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. While StarLiX provides B2B SaaS for compliance and payments, there is no evidence of a specific focus on French SMEs/mid-market or regulated intermediaries within France, and no mention of specific European regulatory frameworks like AIFMD, MiFID II, DORA, or IDD.https://starlix.io
Payaut20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides embedded payment solutions at an enterprise level, which is not an early-stage B2B SaaS nor specifically focused on AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary technology for French SMEs or mid-market companies/regulated intermediaries.https://nuvei.com/embedded-payments-for-platforms
ComplyPay30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed because the company's country of operation is not specified, and the thesis specifically targets French companies. There is no evidence ComplyPay targets French SMEs or mid-market companies or regulated intermediaries in France.https://complypay.com
NORBr B.V.10NO MATCHNORBr B.V. provides payment infrastructure PaaS, which is not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor is it B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries automating reporting/KYC/AML under specified EU frameworks. It offers payment services, which is a different B2B fintech category than the target regulated intermediary focus. Therefore, it fails STEP B.https://norbr.com
Genieverse20NO MATCHThe company's platform automates travel operations, which is outside the specified target verticals of operations, finance, legal, or HR teams for AI workflow automation, and is not within the regulated intermediary fintech sector. Also, the company's country is not stated as France.https://genieverse.travel
MAIC Hospitality Solutions10NO MATCHThe company provides a hospitality-focused software platform, which does not align with the 'AI-native workflow automation' or 'regulated intermediary fintech' verticals specified in STEP B of the gate question.https://maic.lu
Attlaz30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: Attlaz is an iPaaS solution providing integrations and automation for various business functions, which broadly aligns with workflow automation. However, there is no evidence it is AI-native (LLM-powered, agent-based, or generative AI co-pilot) or specifically targets French SMEs/mid-market for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams. It also does not fit the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle. The company's country is not stated, and customer testimonials are European-sounding but not explicitly French.https://attlaz.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Kili Technology (anchor)
2. Nabla (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 2 companies → evaluated: 0 for Profund VC

LinkedIn Posts — Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 9 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run
THIS RUN — 1 entry for Profund VC:
Week 19 → angle: ai_workflow_automationParis | Artificial Intelligence | 1-10 employees
ⓘ operatingStatus=Active · companyType=For Profit · Country auto-filled from city.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 175 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

The company's core business is an early-stage B2B AI-powered regulatory compliance platform (SaaS) sold to enterprise compliance teams, fulfilling STEP A. It aligns with the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle (STEP B) by providing B2B SaaS for compliance officers automating regulatory reporting and workflows under frameworks like DORA and MiFID II, which includes French regulated intermediaries, though the geographical focus on France is not explicitly stated. 📍 WHERE FOUND: websets
🤖 WHY IT MATCHES:
The company provides an AI-powered data platform specializing in financial information for professionals, aligning with the B2B software product criteria (STEP A). 🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
2. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 16d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 17d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 18d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 19d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 20d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 21d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 22d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”

in 23d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 24d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 25d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 26d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”

It specifically matches the 'tmc_workflow_platform' angle (B) by providing a SaaS platform that automates ticketing, PNR management, refunds, and queue automation for GDS-reliant travel sellers and operators, effectively digitizing TMC internal operations and reducing servicing costs per transaction, thereby fulfilling both gate criteria.https://genieverse.travel

This relates to general hotel operations rather than specifically B2B corporate travel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or ERP integrations for business travel, which are the required angles.https://maic.lu
Attlaz20NO MATCHAttlaz is a generic iPaaS solution, providing data unification and automation across various business functions like e-commerce, finance, IT, marketing, and sales. While it offers ERP integrations, it is not specialized for the travel domain and lacks specific features for hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate travel payments, or TMC workflows. Therefore, it fails STEP 1 as it is a generic tool rather than a European corporate travel technology provider.https://attlaz.com
VOGSY20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because VOGSY is a general project-based service business platform that connects opportunities, resources, and financial data, covering global invoicing and reporting. It is not specifically a corporate travel technology solution, but rather a broader professional services automation (PSA) tool. The company does not specialize in hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation for travel, or TMC workflow. Its focus on 'global project-based service businesses' like consulting firms and agencies is too broad to fit the specific corporate travel technology domain.https://vogsy.global

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. FCM Travel (anchor)
2. Cytric Easy (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 2 companies → evaluated: 0 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

Week 19 → angle: hotel_booking_saascorporate hotel booking platform product manager @ Europe

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 9 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
Hacker News — Show HN (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
